skill.name: zhihu-search-api-skill
skill.description: Search Zhihu articles through the BrowserAct API and return structured article details, links, authors, publication dates, images, and full body text.
skill.summary: Extract structured Zhihu article results with BrowserAct workflow automation.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found network calls, environment-variable access, markdown command examples, and a critical combined-risk heuristic. Review confirms expected BrowserAct API use and no prompt injection or malicious exfiltration evidence, but the skill still sends user queries and an API key to a third-party service.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Key and Query Transmission
medium_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and sends authorization headers plus user search parameters to BrowserAct workflow endpoints. This is expected for the skill, but users must understand that keywords and API credentials are handled by a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly builds a Bearer authorization header and sends the payload to BrowserAct. The use appears legitimate for the skill purpose, but the privacy and credential exposure risk is real.

medium_findings.title: Polling and Result Retrieval from External Workflow API
medium_findings.description: The script starts a BrowserAct workflow, polls task status, and retrieves task output over the network. This broadens trust to BrowserAct responses and can expose returned Zhihu content through terminal output.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network polling and result retrieval are explicit and required by the workflow. There is no evidence of hidden destinations, but the external response is trusted and printed.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many SKILL.md lines as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These are markdown inline code spans and fenced examples, not executable code inside the skill runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown documentation and examples. No Ruby backtick execution or dynamic shell interpolation exists in those lines.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Heuristic Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged several SKILL.md lines as weak cryptography, but the cited content describes API parameters and output fields. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic primitive is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain natural-language descriptions and markdown field names. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Heuristic Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance, but the cited code stores a returned BrowserAct task id and the documentation discusses CAPTCHA and IP restrictions. No host, network, or filesystem reconnaissance behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines do not call system inventory commands or inspect local environment details beyond the expected API key check. The scanner likely matched keywords without execution context.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded BrowserAct URLs Are Expected Configuration
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to BrowserAct API and console endpoints documented by the skill. They are not hidden or suspicious destinations, but users should know requests leave the local environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible, consistent with the skill description, and limited to BrowserAct. This supports legitimate configuration rather than covert exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Critical Combined-Risk Heuristic Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static heuristic combined network use, credential access, and documented script execution. Context shows these behaviors support the stated BrowserAct workflow, and no malicious destination, prompt injection, or credential theft evidence was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The combined pattern is real, but semantic review aligns it with the declared third-party API workflow. Confidence is slightly lower because community skills with API keys still need user trust.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment API Key Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment and uses it as a Bearer token. This is necessary for the service but should be disclosed as sensitive credential handling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact environment variable and authorization flow are visible. The pattern is sensitive but expected.

dangerous_patterns.title: External HTTP Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The script performs POST and GET requests to the BrowserAct workflow API. This creates third-party network dependency and sends user-selected search parameters outside the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The requests calls are explicit and all target the configured BrowserAct base URL. No additional hidden endpoint was found.

content.value_statement: Zhihu research often requires repeated searches, article opening, and manual extraction. This skill uses BrowserAct to return structured article details and full text from keyword-based Zhihu searches.
actual_capabilities: Runs a Python script that starts a BrowserAct Zhihu search workflow.
Accepts a keyword, publication date range, and article limit.
Polls BrowserAct task status until completion or timeout.
Returns article titles, authors, dates, links, images, and body content when available.
Prints clear status messages and API-key setup guidance.
Supports one automatic retry when the task fails for non-authorization reasons.
limitations: Requires a valid BROWSERACT_API_KEY before use.
Sends search parameters and authorization to BrowserAct external APIs.
Depends on BrowserAct workflow availability and Zhihu result accessibility.
Does not perform sentiment analysis or deduplication by itself.

use_cases.title: Track Industry Topics
use_cases.description: Find recent Zhihu articles about a market, technology, or policy topic and collect full article content for review.

use_cases.title: Monitor Brand Mentions
use_cases.description: Search recent Zhihu content for a company, product, or campaign and gather article metadata for follow-up analysis.

use_cases.title: Collect Competitor Updates
use_cases.description: Retrieve Zhihu posts about competitors across a defined date range to support product and strategy research.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Topic Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Zhihu for articles about generative AI from the past 7 days. Return up to 10 results with titles, authors, dates, and links.

prompt_templates.title: Brand Monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Zhihu search skill to find articles mentioning our brand from the past 30 days. Include full article text when available.

prompt_templates.title: Competitor Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Zhihu for recent articles about these competitors: Company A, Company B, and Company C. Use the 90 day range and summarize recurring themes.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Research Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a structured research brief from extracted results.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Zhihu for low-altitude economy articles from the past year. Extract up to 20 results, group them by theme, and identify useful source links.

output_examples.input: Search Zhihu for AI agent articles from the past 7 days.
output_examples.output: A list of recent Zhihu articles with each title, author, publication date, original link, and available body text.

output_examples.input: Find recent Zhihu coverage for a product launch.
output_examples.output: A concise research summary supported by extracted article details, source links, and publication dates.

output_examples.input: Collect competitor mentions from the past 90 days.
output_examples.output: Grouped findings showing relevant Zhihu articles, key authors, dates, and links for each competitor term.

best_practices: Confirm the BrowserAct API key is available before running the script.
Use specific keywords and date ranges to reduce irrelevant results.
Review returned article links before using extracted content in reports.
anti_patterns: Do not use sensitive personal data as search keywords unless authorized.
Do not assume BrowserAct output is complete without checking source links.
Do not retry repeatedly after an invalid authorization error.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY to start and poll BrowserAct workflow tasks.

faq.question: What date filters are supported?
faq.answer: The skill documents 7d, 30d, 90d, 1y, and all as publication date options.

faq.question: Can it return full article text?
faq.answer: Yes, it returns body content when BrowserAct provides it in the workflow result.

faq.question: Does it run only inside Zhihu?
faq.answer: No. It calls BrowserAct APIs, which perform the Zhihu search workflow.

faq.question: Is it safe for private research topics?
faq.answer: Use caution. Search terms are sent to BrowserAct, so avoid confidential or regulated topics unless approved.

faq.question: What happens when authorization fails?
faq.answer: The skill tells the user to check the BrowserAct API key and should not retry automatically.
